svg.content: <mxfile host="app.diagrams.net" agent="5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/87.0.4280.88 Safari/537.36" scale="1" border="0"> <diagram id="zjsDRZPgKbeWXKB6thL_" name="Page-1"> <mxGraphModel dx="2211" dy="1191" grid="1" gridSize="10" guides="1" tooltips="1" connect="1" arrows="1" fold="1" page="1" pageScale="1" pageWidth="850" pageHeight="1100" background="#F4F1DE" math="0" shadow="0"> <root> <mxCell id="0" /> <mxCell id="1" parent="0" /> <mxCell id="POLPhCSMSO8E7wKJjYnA-1" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F2CC8F;strokeColor=#E07A5F;fontStyle=1;fontColor=#393C56;" value="<span style="font-size: 16px">Way to create an array in JavaScript</span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="310" x="670" y="40" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="POLPhCSMSO8E7wKJjYnA-3" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F2CC8F;strokeColor=#E07A5F;fontStyle=1;align=left;fontColor=#393C56;" value="<span style="font-size: 16px">Using assignment&nbsp;operator<br><br>var array = [ ];<br>var array2 = [1,2,3,4,5]<br><br></span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="100" width="250" x="10" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="POLPhCSMSO8E7wKJjYnA-5" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F2CC8F;strokeColor=#E07A5F;fontStyle=1;align=left;fontColor=#393C56;" value="<span style="font-size: 16px">Using new operator</span><br><br><span style="font-size: 16px">new Array(length);</span><br><div><span style="font-size: 16px">Creates an array with length set to the number.</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">length &gt; 0 otherwise it will throw error</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px"><br></span></div><div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">var arr = new Array(2);</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">arr; [empty × 2]</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">arr.length; // 2</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">arr[0]; undefined</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">// when we set</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">arr[3] = 10; //It will increase the array size</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">[empty × 3, 10]</span></div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="260" width="390" x="270" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="POLPhCSMSO8E7wKJjYnA-6" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F2CC8F;strokeColor=#E07A5F;fontStyle=1;align=left;fontColor=#393C56;" value="<span style="font-size: 16px">Using Array.from</span><br><br><div><span style="font-size: 16px">var arr = Array.from({length : 2});</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">arr; // [undefined, undefined]</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">arr[0] = 1; arr[1] = 2;</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">var arrCopy = Array.from(arr);</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">arrCopy; // [1,2]</span></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="180" width="270" x="690" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="POLPhCSMSO8E7wKJjYnA-7" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F2CC8F;strokeColor=#E07A5F;fontStyle=1;align=left;fontColor=#393C56;" value="<span style="font-size: 16px">Using spread operator</span><br><br><div><span style="font-size: 16px">ar arr =&nbsp; [1,2,3,4,5]</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">var array2 = [ ...arr ]</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">array2; // [1,2,3,4,5]</span></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="100" width="250" x="1000" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="POLPhCSMSO8E7wKJjYnA-8" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F2CC8F;strokeColor=#E07A5F;fontStyle=1;align=left;fontColor=#393C56;" value="<span style="font-size: 16px">Using Array</span><br><br><div><span style="font-size: 16px">Creates a new array with the arguments as items.</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">The length of the array is set to the number of arguments.</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px"><br></span></div><div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">var array = Array(1,2,3,4,5);</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">array; // [1,2,3,4,5]</span></div></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px"><br></span></div><div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">If we pass single number argument ,&nbsp;</span><span style="font-size: 16px">then the Array constructor will create a new Array of the length equivalent to the argument</span></div></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px"><br></span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px"><div>var array= Array(5);</div><div>array; //[empty x 5]</div><div>If we pass string</div><div>var array= new Array("5");</div><div>array; ["5"]</div><div>To create a Array with single number elemennt ,</div><div>&nbsp;we can use Array.of</div></span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px"><br></span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px"><br></span></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="390" width="470" x="1280" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="POLPhCSMSO8E7wKJjYnA-9" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F2CC8F;strokeColor=#E07A5F;fontStyle=1;align=left;fontColor=#393C56;" value="<span style="font-size: 16px">Using Array.of</span><br><br><div><span style="font-size: 16px">It is similar to Array constructor . But</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">. Array.of(5) → [5]</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">Array(5) → [empty x 5]</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">var array = Array.of(5);</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">array; / [5]</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">var array2 = Array.of(1,2,3,4,5,6, "string");</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 16px">array2; // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, "string"]</span></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="240" width="330" x="1770" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="POLPhCSMSO8E7wKJjYnA-10" edge="1" parent="1" div.span: Creates a new array with the arguments as items.
The length of the array is set to the number of arguments.
If we pass single number argument ,
then the Array constructor will create a new Array of the length equivalent to the argument

span.div: To create a Array with single number elemennt ,

span.font: JavaScript arrays are used to store multiple values in a single variable.

span.style: font-family: verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
span.font: An array is a special variable, which can hold more than one value at a time.
